channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Unable to Access NDTV Education Page for School Assembly News on March 31
item.description: The page from NDTV Education regarding school assembly news headlines for March 31 could not be accessed. Users attempting to visit the link encountered an access denial message indicating that the se...

item.title: Oga Obinna Shares Special Breakfast with Son after 1st Term Ends at Nairobi School
item.description: Kenyan YouTuber Oga Obinna recently created a heartwarming moment with his son, Luirrone, after picking him up from Nairobi School at the close of the first term. The father-son duo enjoyed quality ti...

item.title: Government reviews public institutions’ financial performance and accountability
item.description: Dar es Salaam – President Samia Suluhu Hassan has received three major oversight reports for the 2024/25 financial year from the Controller and Auditor General (CAG), the Prevention and Combating of C...

item.title: Court orders Meesha Shafi to pay damages to Ali Zafar in defamation case
item.description: A Lahore sessions court has ordered singer Meesha Shafi to pay Rs5 million to fellow artist Ali Zafar in a defamation case. The decision, issued on Tuesday, follows years of legal proceedings that beg...

item.title: How to Avoid Airport Mix-Ups When Booking Flights
item.description: When booking flights, it’s important to pay attention to airport codes to avoid unexpected inconveniences or extra costs. Many cities have multiple airports, and budget airlines often use secondary ai...

item.title: Avi Lewis Wins NDP Leadership and Challenges Liberal Party
item.description: The federal NDP has taken a sharp turn to the left with Avi Lewis's decisive first-ballot victory in the party's leadership race. With the NDP currently holding minimal influence in the House of Commo...

item.title: Israel enacts death penalty law targeting Palestinians convicted of deadly attacks
item.description: On Monday, Israel's parliament passed a controversial law making the death penalty by hanging the default sentence for Palestinians convicted in military courts of deadly attacks. Critics highlight th...

item.title: Lahore Court Orders Meesha Shafi to Pay Rs5 Million to Ali Zafar in Defamation Case
item.description: In a significant legal development, a sessions court in Lahore has directed singer Meesha Shafi to pay Rs5 million to fellow singer and actor Ali Zafar in a defamation case. The case, which dates back...

item.title: U.S. Strategist Highlights Stocks Poised for Stability Amid Market Volatility
item.description: In the latest market analysis, Evercore ISI strategist Julian Emanuel identifies a set of stocks that could benefit if U.S. markets experience a period of relative stability. Despite ongoing concerns ...

item.title: Maharashtra BJP MLA Nitesh Rane faces complaint over alleged inflammatory remarks at Ram Navami procession
item.description: A complaint has been registered against Maharashtra minister and BJP leader Nitesh Rane for allegedly delivering hate speech during a Ram Navami procession in Mumbai's Malvani area. Resident Shanul Sy...

item.title: IHC adjourns hearing on Imran Khan and Bushra Bibi’s sentence suspension pleas
item.description: On Tuesday, the Islamabad High Court (IHC) postponed the hearing of the suspension of sentence applications filed by former Prime Minister Imran Khan and his spouse Bushra Bibi in the £190 million gra...

item.title: Pakistan’s Mediation Role in Iran-Israel Conflict Explained
item.description: The article discusses Pakistan's emerging position as a mediator in the ongoing Iran-Israel tensions. Analysts note that Pakistan’s diplomatic engagement should not come as a surprise, given its histo...

item.title: Armed Men Raid EKSU Hostels, Students Flee for Safety
item.description: Late Monday night, serious wahala burst for Ado-Ekiti as armed men attack the off-campus hostels for Ekiti State University (EKSU) students. Plenty students run for their lives as the gunmen waka from...

item.title: Kristoffer Borgli’s The Drama blends romance and satire in a dark wedding tale with Zendaya and Robert Pattinson
item.description: Kristoffer Borgli’s latest film, *The Drama*, is a darkly comic, unsettling exploration of love, secrets, and the limits of moral comfort. The story follows Charlie, a rather shy British art historian...

item.title: EV Charging Companies Face Sharp Rise in Fixed Energy Charges, Warn of Higher Costs for Drivers and Risks to UK EV Transition
item.description: Electric vehicle charger operators in the UK are confronting dramatic increases in their energy bills due to changes implemented by the energy regulator Ofgem in 2023. The regulator altered how standi...

item.title: Scott Mills leaves BBC Radio 2 amid historic sexual offence investigation
item.description: Scott Mills, a long-standing BBC presenter, has been dismissed from BBC Radio 2 following allegations related to his personal conduct. The allegations concern a historic relationship with a teenage bo...

item.title: Fair Work Commission to phase out junior pay rates for workers over 18
item.description: The Fair Work Commission has announced a significant change to youth wages in Australia. Starting this December, adults aged 18 to 20 with at least six months of experience in fast food, retail, and p...

item.title: US and Israel launch multiple airstrikes on Iran, hitting military and civilian sites
item.description: Na today, reports don come say US and Israeli forces don launch plenty airstrikes for Iran, targeting military bases, a plant wey dey produce cancer medicine, and even one Shia religious site. Video d...

item.title: Canadian Transportation Agency considers lowering airline complaints fee amid industry lobbying
item.description: The Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA) plans to reduce a $790 fee that was intended to make airlines contribute to the cost of handling air passenger complaints, following lobbying efforts by the ai...

item.title: SA Government Cuts Fuel Tax by R3 to Ease Costs Amid Oil Price Surge
item.description: Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana has announced a reduction of R3 per litre in the fuel tax for both petrol and diesel, aiming to ease the burden on South Africans facing high fuel costs. The announce...
